title: Impact Fit Fail for Majority of Parameters and One-Sided Fit

posts.name: Jihoon Shin
posts.text:
I am currently doing unblinded Impact plot and Goodness-of-Fit Test.
While doing the nuisance impact, I found two categories of problems.
1. of ~600 nuisance parameters, ~500 of them fail the fitting and not shown in impact plots.
2. One-sided nuisance fit

impacts_El_MN95_SOff.pdf (23.7 KB)
impacts_El_MN40_SOff.pdf (185.4 KB)

This is the command I use at the moment:
combineTool.py -M Impacts -d Input_LEPTON_MASS_FullRun2.root -m 125 --doInitialFit --robustFit 1 --rMin -5 --rMax 5 --setParameters r=1 --verbose 3
combineTool.py -M Impacts -d Input_LEPTON_MASS_FullRun2.root -m 125 --doFits --robustFit 1 --X-rtd MINIMIZER_MaxCalls=9999999 --X-rtd MINIMIZER_multiMin_hideConstants=1  --cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy=1 --setRobustFitTolerance 0.01 --cminFallbackAlgo Minuit2,Migrad,0:0.01 --rMin -10 --rMax 10 --job-mode condor --dry-run --stepSize 0.01 --setParameters r=1 --cminPreScan

I am trying different options to fix these issues. Three things I can think of and am trying are
1. Increase autoMCStats threshold: * autoMCStats 10 0 —> *autoMCStats 20 0
2. try different initial point of r
4. try different r range
I think autoMCStats threshold 10 is the common value used in CMS, but is it okay to use higher threshold, where I have bins with small number of events?
Additionally, if these issues are already well-known, do you have any advice on things I can try, so I do not waste time trying meaningless adjustments?
Thank you.
——————————————————————————
Sincerely,
Jihoon Shin
Seoul National University
jihoon.shin@cern.ch
sjhsjs987@snu.ac.kr
——————————————————————————
posts.cooked_html:
I am currently doing unblinded Impact plot and Goodness-of-Fit Test.

While doing the nuisance impact, I found two categories of problems.

1. of ~600 nuisance parameters, ~500 of them fail the fitting and not shown in impact plots.
2. One-sided nuisance fit
  1. impacts_El_MN95_SOff.pdf (23.7 KB)
  2. impacts_El_MN40_SOff.pdf (185.4 KB)

This is the command I use at the moment:

combineTool.py -M Impacts -d Input_LEPTON_MASS_FullRun2.root -m 125 --doInitialFit --robustFit 1 --rMin -5 --rMax 5 --setParameters r=1 --verbose 3
combineTool.py -M Impacts -d Input_LEPTON_MASS_FullRun2.root -m 125 --doFits --robustFit 1 --X-rtd MINIMIZER_MaxCalls=9999999 --X-rtd MINIMIZER_multiMin_hideConstants=1  --cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy=1 --setRobustFitTolerance 0.01 --cminFallbackAlgo Minuit2,Migrad,0:0.01 --rMin -10 --rMax 10 --job-mode condor --dry-run --stepSize 0.01 --setParameters r=1 --cminPreScan

I am trying different options to fix these issues. Three things I can think of and am trying are
1. Increase autoMCStats threshold: * autoMCStats 10 0 —> *autoMCStats 20 0
2. try different initial point of r
4. try different r range

I think autoMCStats threshold 10 is the common value used in CMS, but is it okay to use higher threshold, where I have bins with small number of events?

Additionally, if these issues are already well-known, do you have any advice on things I can try, so I do not waste time trying meaningless adjustments?

Thank you.

——————————————————————————
Sincerely,
Jihoon Shin
Seoul National University
jihoon.shin@cern.ch
sjhsjs987@snu.ac.kr

posts.name: Massimiliano Galli
posts.text:
Hi Jihoon,
did you manage to find a solution for this? If not, could you please attach the datacard and input file, such that it can be reproduced?
Thank you,
Massimiliano
